question: Synonym of ASSUAGE

Prohibit : formally forbid (something) by law, rule, or other authority. Ease : absence of difficulty or effort. Enlarge : make or become larger or more extensive. Assuage : make (an unpleasant feeling) less intense. Synonym of Assuage is Ease

question: Synonym of STAMINA
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Endurance : the ability to endure an unpleasant or difficult process or situation without giving way. Intensity : the quality of being intense. Eagerness : enthusiasm to do or to have something; keenness. Energy : the strength and vitality required for sustained physical or mental activity. Stamina : the ability to sustain prolonged physical or mental effort. Synonym of Stamina is Endurance

question: Synonym of MORIBUND
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Stagnant : (of a body of water or the atmosphere of a confined space) having no current or flow and often having an unpleasant smell as a consequence. Gloomy : dark or poorly lit, especially so as to appear depressing or frightening. Dying : on the point of death. Superfluous : unnecessary, especially through being more than enough. Moribund : (of a person) at the point of death. Synonym of Moribund is Dying

question: Synonym of MURKY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Ugly : unpleasant or repulsive, especially in appearance. Dishonest : behaving or prone to behave in an untrustworthy, deceitful, or insincere way. Dull : lacking brightness, vividness, or sheen. Lazy : unwilling to work or use energy. Murky : dark and gloomy, especially due to thick mist. Synonym of Murky is Dull

question: Synonym of VAPID
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Virtuous : having or showing high moral standards. Priceless : so precious that its value cannot be determined. Insipid : lacking vigour or interest. Vital : absolutely necessary; essential. Vapid : offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging; bland. Synonym of Vapid is Insipid

question: Synonym of SOZZLED
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Burnt : (of a fire) flame or glow while consuming a material such as coal or wood. Cultured : characterized by refined taste and manners and good education. Drunk : affected by alcohol to the extent of losing control of one's faculties or behaviour. Moderate : average in amount, intensity, quality, or degree. Sozzled : very drunk. Synonym of Sozzled is Drunk

question: Synonym of OCCULT
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Mysterious : difficult or impossible to understand, explain, or identify. Harmless : not able or likely to cause harm. Dreadful : causing or involving great suffering, fear, or unhappiness; extremely bad or serious. Amazing : causing great surprise or wonder; astonishing. Occult : mystical, supernatural, or magical powers, practices, or phenomena. Synonym of Occult is Mysterious

question: Synonym of VERATE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Scold : remonstrate with or rebuke (someone) angrily. Judge : a public officer appointed to decide cases in a law court. Downgrade : reduce to a lower grade, rank, or level of importance. Deny : state that one refuses to admit the truth or existence of. Rebuke : express sharp disapproval or criticism of (someone) because of their behaviour or actions. Synonym of Rebuke is Scold

question: Synonym of DEMISE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Decline : (typically of something regarded as good) become smaller, fewer, or less; decrease. Decay : (of organic matter) rot or decompose through the action of bacteria and fungi. Dormant : (of an animal) having normal physical functions suspended or slowed down for a period of time; in or as if in a deep sleep. End : a termination of a state or situation. Demise : the end or failure of an enterprise or institution. Synonym of Demise is End

question: Synonym of BENEFACTOR
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Guardian : a person who protects or defends something. Saint : a person acknowledged as holy or virtuous and regarded in Christian faith as being in heaven after death. Patron : a person who gives financial or other support to a person, organization, or cause. Friend : a person with whom one has a bond of mutual affection, typically one exclusive of sexual or family relations. Benefactor : a person who gives money or other help to a person or cause. Synonym of Benefactor is Patron

question: Synonym of JINK
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Inherit : receive (money, property, or a title) as an heir at the death of the previous holder. Refrain : stop oneself from doing something. Dodge : move quickly to one side or out of the way. Travel : make a journey, typically of some length. Jink : change direction suddenly and nimbly, as when dodging a pursuer. Synonym of Jink is Dodge

question: Synonym of ELICIT
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Induce : succeed in persuading or leading (someone) to do something. Extract : obtain (something such as money or information) from someone unwilling to give it. Divulge : make known (private or sensitive information). Instil : gradually but firmly establish (an idea or attitude) in a person's mind. Elicit : evoke or draw out (a reaction, answer, or fact) from someone. Synonym of Elicit is Extract

question: Synonym of NIGGARD
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Miser : a person who hoards wealth and spends as little money as possible. Loyal : giving or showing firm and constant support or allegiance to a person or institution. Divine : of or like God or a god. Shrewd : having or showing sharp powers of judgement; astute. Niggard : a mean or ungenerous person; a miser. Synonym of Niggard is Miser

question: Synonym of THWARTED
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Accepted : generally believed or recognized to be valid or correct. Opposed : anxious to prevent or put an end to; disagreeing with. Diverted : cause (someone or something) to change course or turn from one direction to another. Advocated : publicly recommend or support. Thwarted : oppose (a plan, attempt, or ambition) successfully. Synonym of Thwarted is Opposed

question: Synonym of ENCUMBRANCE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Disturbance : the interruption of a settled and peaceful condition. Burden : a duty or misfortune that causes worry, hardship, or distress. Distraction : a thing that prevents someone from concentrating on something else. Diversion : the action of turning something aside from its course. Encumbrance : an impediment or burden. Synonym of Encumbrance is Burden

question: Synonym of WRANGLE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Competition : the activity or condition of striving to gain or win something by defeating or establishing superiority over others. Race : a competition between runners, horses, vehicles, etc. to see which is the fastest in covering a set course. Dispute : a disagreement or argument. Wrestling : the sport or activity of grappling with an opponent and trying to throw or hold them down on the ground, typically according to a code of rules. Wrangle : a dispute or argument, typically one that is long and complicated. Synonym of Wrangle is Dispute

question: Synonym of OBSCENE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Objectionable : arousing distaste or opposition; unpleasant or offensive. Indecent : not conforming with generally accepted standards of behaviour, especially in relation to sexual matters. Displeasing : causing annoyance or dissatisfaction; disagreeable. Condemn : express complete disapproval of; censure. Obscene : (of the portrayal or description of sexual matters) offensive or disgusting by accepted standards of morality and decency. Synonym of Obscene is Indecent

question: Synonym of SUPERVENE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Ensue : happen or occur afterwards or as a result. Interfere : intervene in a situation without invitation or necessity. Displace : take over the place, position, or role of. Precede : come before (something) in time. Supervene : occur as an interruption or change to an existing situation. Synonym of Supervene is Interfere

question: Synonym of BELITTLE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Diminish : make or become less. Mock : tease or laugh at in a scornful or contemptuous manner. Disparage : regard or represent as being of little worth. Scoff : speak to someone or about something in a scornfully derisive or mocking way. Belittle : dismiss (someone or something) as unimportant. Synonym of Belittle is Disparage

question: Synonym of HEDIOUS
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Secret : not known or seen or not meant to be known or seen by others. Loathsome : causing hatred or disgust; repulsive. Disliked : feel distaste for or hostility towards. Hidden : kept out of sight; concealed. Hideous : extremely unpleasant. Synonym of Hideous is Loathsome

question: Synonym of KNAVERY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Heroism : great bravery. Cowardice : lack of bravery. Dishonesty : deceitfulness shown in someone's character or behaviour. Stupidity : behaviour that shows a lack of good sense or judgement. Knavery: unprincipled, untrustworthy, or dishonest dealing. Synonym of Knavery is Dishonesty

question: Synonym of IRONIC
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Good-natured : kind, friendly, and patient. Inflexible : unwilling to change or compromise. Sarcastic : marked by or given to using irony in order to mock or convey contempt. Bitter : having a sharp, pungent taste or smell; not sweet. Ironic : using or characterized by irony. Synonym of Ironic is Sarcastic

question: Synonym of MOTLEY
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Variegated : exhibiting different colours, especially as irregular patches or streaks. Active : engaging or ready to engage in physically energetic pursuits. Disguised : having changed one's appearance in order to conceal one's identity. Somber : dark or dull in colour or tone. Motley : (of clothing) made up of a variety of colours. Synonym of Motley is Variegated
